transcript: Did you know the average person should consume between 9 and 13 cups of water in a day? That may sound overwhelming, but it's dependent on your age, activity level, and some medications that you take. I find water is super beneficial when you're trying to lose weight. Drinking it throughout the day helps to keep you full for no calories. Here are five tips to trick you into drinking more water throughout the day. I find that drinking more water is super easy to do when you carry a tall drinking glass or a bottle with you. Sometimes one that fits easily into a purse or a backpack can be a simple solution. Consider trying zero calorie drink mixes. They come in powdered and liquid options and are easy to take on the go. Looking for a more subtle flavor? Try adding fresh produce to your water. Lemons, limes, and even cucumbers give just a hint of flavor to a nice ice water. Unsweetened tea is a great option. All varieties, as long as it's unsweetened, is super hydrating and definitely provides an antioxidant benefit. Tea can have a diuretic effect, which means you might be using the bathroom more, but new studies show it's just as hydrating as water. Find normal water boring? You're in luck. Sparkling water is everywhere these days and it still keeps you super hydrated. But be sure to check the label. You want to look for a sugar-free variety with no sodium for the best hydration option.
